title: Between Glimpses of Hope and Catastrophic Danger: Mankind Was Born for That Which Is Better
description:
IPC #148 Friday, April 3, 11am ET/ 5pm CET

RSVP for Zoom link
https://schillerinstitute.nationbuilder.com/ipc_meeting

On the evening of April 1, NASA’s Artemis II crew successfully launched from Cape Canaveral, Florida, sending human beings to orbit the Moon for the first time in fifty-four years. Although a rarity in recent years, this launch truly represents an accomplishment for the world, and a reminder that humanity was made for a more important purpose than pointless wars and endless bickering. As astronaut Jeremy Hansen said in a pre-launch message moments before the countdown began: “We are going for all humanity.”

Meanwhile, back in the land of the clueless, U.S. President Donald Trump was forced to respond the growing pressure—both internally and from around the world—against his illegal and disastrous war in Iran. In an extremely defensive April 1 address to the nation, Trump said that the U.S. is “on track to complete all of America’s military objectives shortly,” claiming that Iran has been “eviscerated and essentially is really no longer a threat.” Cynically, he said that this war of aggression was “necessary for the safety of America and the security of the free world,” making sure to say the words “nuclear weapon” repeatedly in order to induce fear among the gullible. While still no clear objective exists, the closest thing was Trump’s statement that: “We’re going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We’re going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. In the meantime, discussions are ongoing…. Yet, if during this period of time no deal is made, we have our eyes on key targets. If there is no deal, we are going to hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously.”

In her weekly webcast on April 1, Helga Zepp-LaRouche said that Trump “is in a real bind, because obviously, almost five weeks into the war, all his objectives have not been met.” All his attempts to portray victory and resolve are merely “a desperate attempt by Trump to somehow control the narrative when all the realities on the ground are speaking in a completely different direction.” But no matter what, his posturing “will not be believed in the Global Majority, it will not be believed by the American population,” she warned.

Read full invite here: https://schillerinstitute.nationbuilder.com/ipc_meeting
Join EIR round table on Monday, April 6: https://eir.news/2026/03/news/eir-announces-april-6-emergency-roundtable-a-dialogue-of-civilizations-is-there-still-time-to-prevent-the-war-against-iranfrom-escalating-into-a-global-nuclear-conflict/
